adds.rationale: Replace URA's pure uranium-miner exposure with NUKZ's broader nuclear-renaissance coverage — advanced-reactor developers, utilities, construction/services, and fuel — to capture the AI-data-center baseload demand narrative that moved from speculation to Goldman Sachs institutional consensus by Q2 2024.

news_evidence.summary: NUKZ launched Jan 24, 2024 on NYSE Arca tracking the VettaFi Nuclear Renaissance Index; >=80% in nuclear companies spanning reactor developers, utilities, construction, and fuel — broader than URA's uranium-miner focus and aligned to the SMR/PPA thesis.

Goldman Sachs (May 2024) projected AI driving a 160% increase in US data-center power demand by 2030, naming nuclear a must-have baseload — the thesis NUKZ's utility/SMR holdings target and URA's miners structurally cannot.
Oklo went public May 2024 via SPAC (~$850M); its Aurora fast-fission reactor targets commercial customers — the forward SMR pipeline NUKZ holds and URA misses.

removes.rationale: URA's two primary catalysts — uranium spot at a 15-year high in Feb 2024 and the Russian uranium import ban signed May 13, 2024 — are now enacted and priced into miner valuations; NUKZ replaces URA with a more forward-looking renaissance basket aligned to the AI-data-center electricity thesis.

news_evidence.summary: Uranium spot peaked ~$106/lb in Feb 2024 (15-year high) then pulled back as near-term supply improved, removing the sharp commodity tailwind behind URA's miner-heavy portfolio.

Biden signed the Prohibiting Russian Uranium Imports Act on May 13, 2024, banning Russian LEU imports and unlocking $2.72B for domestic enrichment — the primary policy catalyst behind early-2024 miner outperformance, now fully enacted.

rationale_overall: Single swap — URA out, NUKZ in — upgrades the nuclear bucket from a commodity-mining expression to a full renaissance basket matching the profile's 'fission renaissance via SMRs and long-term PPAs to AI data centers' thesis. URA's key catalysts (Feb uranium peak, May Russian ban) are priced in; NUKZ adds the reactor-developer and utility layer for the next leg. Goldman's May 2024 data-center power report and Oklo's SPAC debut confirm the AI-nuclear narrative crossed into institutional consensus. Seven positions kept: AI (NVDA $26B Q1 FY25 on May 22 + 10:1 split June 10, MSFT, GOOGL), RKLB (Neutron buildout, intact Electron cadence), BOTZ and QTUM (early-buildup robotics and quantum), ITA (defense thesis intact amid Ukraine and rising NATO spending).
